title: Internal Component Error
shortDescription: A critical internal hardware error has occurred in the printer.
fullDescription: Error 0x97 on Epson printers indicates a fatal internal hardware error, often related to the mainboard or printhead assembly. The printer will not function until the error is cleared or the component is replaced.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 5 minutes, then plug back in.

fixSteps.title: Remove Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer and remove any paper jams or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect USB
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug all USB cables, power cycle, then reconnect.

fixSteps.title: Clean Printhead Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the printhead and gently clean the electrical contacts with a lint-free cloth dampened with distilled water.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling and cleaning contacts does not resolve the error.

faq.a: Sometimes power cycling works. If not, the printhead or mainboard may need replacement.
If the printer cost under $200, replacement is often more economical than board repair.
Power surges, age, and printhead wear are common causes.

title: Scanner Unit Error
shortDescription: The scanner unit has encountered a hardware error.
fullDescription: Error 0x9A indicates the scanner mechanism or sensor has failed. This affects scanning, copying, and any function that requires the scanner unit.

fixSteps.title: Clean Glass
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the scanner glass and underside of the lid with glass cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Check for Objects
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanner and check for paper clips, staples, or paper scraps.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check Cable
fixSteps.instruction: If comfortable, open the unit and verify the scanner flat cable is properly seated.

whenToCallPro: If the scanner motor makes grinding noises or the cable appears damaged.

faq.q: Can I still print with this error?
faq.a: On some models, printing may still work even when scanning is disabled.
Yes, but it requires disassembly — often not cost-effective on budget models.
Age, mechanical wear, and foreign objects are the main causes.

title: Paper Feed Error
shortDescription: The paper feed mechanism cannot pick up or advance paper.
fullDescription: Error 0xEA indicates the paper feed rollers are unable to grip and feed paper. This is typically caused by worn or dirty rollers, improper paper loading, or a paper jam.

fixSteps.title: Reload Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Remove all paper, fan the stack, and reload it properly in the tray.

fixSteps.title: Clean Rollers
fixSteps.instruction: Dampen a lint-free cloth with water and gently wipe the paper feed rollers.

fixSteps.title: Run Cleaning Utility
fixSteps.instruction: Access the printer menu and run the paper feed roller cleaning utility if available.

fixSteps.title: Check for Debris
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the paper path for torn paper, labels, or debris.

whenToCallPro: If rollers are visibly worn and cleaning does not help.

faq.a: Roller replacement kits are available for some models — check Epson support.

faq.q: What type of paper works best?
faq.a: Use standard 20lb copy paper. Glossy or thick paper can cause feed issues.

faq.q: How often should I clean rollers?
faq.a: Every few months or when you notice feed problems.

title: General Printer Error
shortDescription: A general hardware error has occurred.
fullDescription: Error E-01 is a generic catch-all error on Epson printers indicating an internal hardware fault. It often requires a power cycle or service.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and check for paper jams, debris, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Update Driver
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest driver from Epson's support website.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset through the printer's settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Epson error E-01 mean?
faq.a: A general hardware error has occurred.
This is a frequently reported error on Epson printers.
If the printer is under 3 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older printers may be more economical to replace.

title: Paper Jam Detected
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer.
fullDescription: Error E-02 indicates paper is stuck in the paper path. The printer will not operate until the jam is completely cleared.

causes.cause: Printer not registered with Epson Connect

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and check for paper jams, debris, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Update Driver
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest driver from Epson's support website.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset through the printer's settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Epson error E-600 mean?
faq.a: The printer cannot connect to Epson Connect cloud services.
This is a frequently reported error on Epson printers.
If the printer is under 3 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older printers may be more economical to replace.

title: Fax Communication Error
shortDescription: The fax function failed to send or receive.
fullDescription: Error E-700 indicates a failure in the fax communication process — the call could not be completed or the handshake with the remote fax failed.

causes.cause: Remote fax machine busy or offline

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 60 seconds, and turn it back on.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open all covers and check for paper jams, debris, or foreign objects.

fixSteps.title: Update Driver
fixSteps.instruction: Download and install the latest driver from Epson's support website.

fixSteps.title: Factory Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Perform a factory reset through the printer's settings menu.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after power cycling and basic troubleshooting.

faq.q: What does Epson error E-700 mean?
faq.a: The fax function failed to send or receive.
This is a frequently reported error on Epson printers.
If the printer is under 3 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. Older printers may be more economical to replace.

title: Paper Jam or No Paper Fed
shortDescription: Paper is jammed inside the printer or the feed mechanism failed to pick up paper.
fullDescription:
Error E-03 on Epson printers indicates either a paper jam inside the printer or a failure of the paper feed mechanism to pick up paper from the tray. This is one of the most common Epson printer errors.

The error can be caused by torn paper remnants from a previous jam, a dirty or worn pickup roller, paper loaded incorrectly, or paper that is too thick, thin, or curled for the feed mechanism.

When this error occurs, the printer stops all operations and requires the jam to be cleared or the feed issue to be resolved before printing can resume.
symptoms: Error E-03 on LCD or computer

fixSteps.title: Remove Jammed Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer. Open the front cover and rear cover (if applicable). Gently pull any jammed paper out in the direction of paper travel.

fixSteps.title: Check for Paper Scraps
fixSteps.instruction: Look inside the paper path with a flashlight for any small torn pieces of paper. Remove all remnants.

fixSteps.title: Reload Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Fan the paper stack, align edges evenly, and reload into the tray. Adjust side guides to touch paper without bending.

fixSteps.title: Clean Pickup Roller
fixSteps.instruction: Dampen a lint-free cloth with water and gently clean the rubber pickup roller visible inside the paper tray area.

whenToCallPro: If jams recur frequently after cleaning and the pickup roller appears worn smooth, professional roller replacement may be needed.

faq.a: Repeated jams usually indicate a worn pickup roller, incorrect paper type, or humid paper. Try fresh paper and clean the rollers.

faq.q: Can I print from a different tray?
faq.a: If your model has multiple paper sources, try selecting a different tray in the print settings.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Recognized
shortDescription: One or more ink cartridges are not recognized by the printer.
fullDescription:
Error E-04 on Epson printers indicates the printer cannot communicate with or recognize one or more installed ink cartridges. The printer reads cartridge identification data through electronic contacts on each cartridge.

This error commonly occurs with newly installed cartridges that have dirty contacts, non-genuine or refilled cartridges with incompatible chips, or cartridges that are not properly seated in their slots.

The printer will not allow printing until all cartridge positions show as recognized, as it needs to monitor ink levels and cartridge health for proper operation.
symptoms: Error E-04 on LCD or computer

fixSteps.title: Reseat Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge cover, remove the unrecognized cartridge(s), and press them firmly back in until they click.

fixSteps.title: Clean Contacts
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the cartridge and gently wipe the gold IC chip contacts with a dry lint-free cloth. Clean the matching contacts inside the printer.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 2 minutes, then turn on. The printer re-reads cartridges during startup.

fixSteps.title: Try Genuine Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: If using third-party cartridges, install a genuine Epson cartridge to test if the printer accepts it.

whenToCallPro: If genuine Epson cartridges are still not recognized after cleaning, the printer's cartridge reader may need repair.

faq.a: Epson printers verify cartridge chips for compatibility. Some firmware updates may change which cartridges are accepted.

faq.q: Do I need to replace all cartridges?
faq.a: No, only the unrecognized cartridge(s) need attention. The error indicates which specific cartridge has the issue.

title: Ink Cartridge Not Installed
shortDescription: One or more required ink cartridges are missing from the printer.
fullDescription:
Error E-05 on Epson printers indicates that one or more ink cartridge slots are empty. The printer requires all cartridge positions to be filled for operation, even if you only intend to print in black and white.

This error typically appears when a cartridge has been removed and not replaced, when a cartridge has come loose from its slot, or when the printer fails to detect a physically present cartridge due to a mechanical issue.

Epson printers monitor all cartridge positions continuously and will not allow any printing when a slot is empty, as running the printhead without all inks present can cause air intake and damage.

fixSteps.title: Install Missing Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: Identify which slot is empty from the printer display or Epson Status Monitor on your computer. Install the correct cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Reseat All Cartridges
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cartridge cover and press each cartridge down firmly until it clicks to ensure all are properly seated.

fixSteps.title: Check Locking Lever
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the cartridge locking lever (if present on your model) is pushed down into the locked position.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn the printer off and on to force a cartridge re-detection cycle.

whenToCallPro: This error rarely requires professional service. If a properly installed cartridge is still not detected, the cartridge slot mechanism may need repair.

faq.q: Can I print in black only without color cartridges?
faq.a: No, Epson printers require all cartridge slots to be filled. This prevents air from entering the printhead ink channels.

faq.q: Why does my cartridge keep popping out?
faq.a: The locking mechanism may be worn or the cartridge may be slightly wrong model. Verify the cartridge model number matches your printer.

title: Ink Pad End of Life
shortDescription: The printer's ink waste pad has reached its capacity limit.
fullDescription:
Error E-06 on Epson printers indicates the internal ink waste pads have reached their estimated capacity. These pads absorb excess ink from printhead cleaning cycles, nozzle checks, and initial ink charging.

When the ink pad counter reaches its limit, the printer stops operating to prevent ink overflow that could damage the printer or stain surfaces. The counter is based on estimated ink usage from cleaning cycles rather than actual measured pad saturation.

This is a common end-of-life error on Epson inkjet printers that have been used for several years. The ink pads can be replaced, and the counter can be reset, but both are typically required.

causes.cause: Normal wear from extended printer use
Counter reached limit (pads may not be fully saturated)

symptoms: Power and ink lights flashing alternately

fixSteps.title: Confirm Error
fixSteps.instruction: Check the Epson Status Monitor on your computer to confirm the ink pad end-of-life message. Note your printer model.

fixSteps.title: Contact Epson Support
fixSteps.instruction: Epson may offer a waste ink pad replacement service. Contact Epson support to inquire about service options for your model.

fixSteps.title: Use Epson Maintenance Reset Utility
fixSteps.instruction: For some models, Epson provides a utility to reset the ink pad counter once. This is a one-time reset per printer.

fixSteps.title: Professional Pad Replacement
fixSteps.instruction: Have the ink waste pads physically replaced by a technician and the counter reset. This restores full printer function.

whenToCallPro: For most users, professional service is recommended as replacing ink pads requires significant disassembly and proper counter reset.

faq.q: Can I reset the counter myself?
faq.a: Epson provides a one-time reset utility for some models. Third-party reset tools exist but are not endorsed by Epson and may void warranty.
If the printer is otherwise working well, pad replacement and reset is much cheaper than a new printer. If the printer is old and has other issues, replacement may be better.

title: Ink Pad Nearing End of Life
shortDescription: The printer's ink waste pads are nearing their capacity limit.
fullDescription:
Error E-07 is a warning that the ink waste pads are nearing their estimated end of life but have not yet reached it. Unlike E-06 which stops the printer, E-07 is a warning that allows continued printing.

This warning gives users advance notice to plan for ink pad service before the printer reaches the E-06 hard stop. The amount of remaining capacity varies by model but is typically 10-20% of the total pad life.

Users should start planning for service when this warning appears, as the transition from E-07 to E-06 can happen at any time depending on how frequently cleaning cycles are performed.

causes.cause: Normal ink pad usage approaching limit
Frequent cleaning cycles accelerating pad wear

fixSteps.title: Acknowledge Warning
fixSteps.instruction: Press OK or Resume to dismiss the warning. The printer will continue to operate.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Cleaning Frequency
fixSteps.instruction: Avoid running unnecessary printhead cleaning cycles. Each cleaning uses the ink pads. Only clean when print quality actually degrades.

fixSteps.title: Plan for Service
fixSteps.instruction: Contact Epson support or a local repair shop to schedule ink pad replacement before the E-06 hard stop occurs.

fixSteps.title: Check Reset Utility
fixSteps.instruction: Visit Epson's support website to check if a free ink pad counter reset utility is available for your printer model.

whenToCallPro: Schedule professional ink pad replacement before the warning escalates to a hard stop (E-06).

faq.q: How long can I keep printing after E-07?
faq.a: It depends on how often cleaning cycles run. You may get weeks to months of additional use. Plan for service promptly.
Yes, the warning will appear each time the printer is powered on or periodically during use until the pads are serviced.

title: Scanner Error
shortDescription: The scanner unit has encountered an error and cannot operate.
fullDescription:
Error E-08 on Epson multifunction printers indicates a scanner hardware error. The scanner mechanism includes a sensor bar that travels along a rail underneath the glass to capture images.

This error is triggered when the scanner sensor cannot move properly, the scanner lamp cannot illuminate, or the sensor readings are abnormal. Common causes include a locked scanner carriage (shipping lock), obstructions on the scanner rail, or a failed scanner motor.

The scanner error typically affects scanning and copying functions but may allow printing to continue normally on some models.

fixSteps.title: Check Scanner Lock
fixSteps.instruction: Check the bottom or side of the scanner for a transportation lock switch. Slide it to the unlocked position.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, unplug for 3 minutes, then restart. The scanner reinitializes during startup.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the scanner lid and look for any objects that may have fallen onto the scanner glass or into the scanner mechanism.

fixSteps.title: Test Print Function
fixSteps.instruction: Try printing a document to verify the print function works independently of the scanner.

whenToCallPro: If the scanner motor or lamp has failed, professional repair or replacement is needed.

faq.q: Can I still print with a scanner error?
faq.a: On most Epson multifunction printers, yes. The print function operates independently of the scanner.
Some models have a physical lock that holds the scanner carriage during shipping. Check your setup guide for its location.

title: Line Fax Communication Error
shortDescription: The fax function has encountered a communication error on the phone line.
fullDescription:
Error E-09 on Epson multifunction printers with fax capability indicates a communication failure during fax transmission or reception. The fax modem could not establish or maintain a connection with the remote fax machine.

This error can be caused by poor phone line quality, no dial tone detected, incompatible fax protocols between machines, or a disconnected phone line. It can also occur when trying to fax through a VoIP (Voice over IP) connection which may not support fax protocols reliably.

The error is specific to the fax function and does not affect printing, scanning, or copying capabilities.

causes.cause: VoIP line not supporting fax protocol

symptoms: Fax fails to send or receive

fixSteps.title: Check Phone Line
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the phone line is connected to the LINE port (not EXT) on the printer. Ensure the other end is connected to an active phone jack.

fixSteps.title: Test Dial Tone
fixSteps.instruction: Connect a standard phone to the line to verify there is a dial tone. If no dial tone, contact your phone service provider.

fixSteps.title: Reduce Fax Speed
fixSteps.instruction: In fax settings, reduce the transmission speed to 9600 bps or lower. This can help with noisy lines or VoIP connections.

fixSteps.title: Disable ECM
fixSteps.instruction: Try disabling Error Correction Mode (ECM) in fax settings. While this reduces error checking, it can help establish connections on poor lines.

whenToCallPro: If the fax modem itself has failed (no fax functions work on a known-good phone line), the modem board may need professional replacement.

faq.a: Fax over VoIP is unreliable. T.38 fax protocol support on your VoIP service can improve reliability. Consider using online fax services instead.

faq.q: Why does the fax work sometimes but not always?
faq.a: Line noise and signal quality vary. Reducing fax speed and disabling ECM can improve reliability on marginal lines.

title: Paper Feed Motor Error
shortDescription: The paper feed motor has failed or is not responding to commands.
fullDescription:
Error code 000020 on Epson printers indicates a failure in the paper feed motor system. This motor drives the rollers that transport paper through the print zone. When the motor cannot start or maintain proper speed, the printer cannot advance paper.

The error can be caused by a mechanical obstruction preventing the motor from turning, a failed motor, a motor driver circuit failure on the main board, or excessive load from jammed paper.

This is a more serious error than a simple paper jam, as it indicates the motor system itself is not functioning rather than just paper being stuck.

causes.cause: Paper jam creating excessive motor load

symptoms: Error 000020 on display or status monitor

fixSteps.title: Clear All Paper
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, remove all paper from trays, and check inside for any jammed paper. Remove any debris.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the printer for 5 minutes to reset motor controller. Plug back in and power on.

fixSteps.title: Check Feed Path
fixSteps.instruction: Open all access doors and manually check that internal rollers turn freely. Remove any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Listen for Motor
fixSteps.instruction: Power on with no paper loaded and listen for motor operation. If silence where motor sound should be, the motor may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the motor or motor driver circuit has failed, professional board-level or motor replacement is required.

faq.q: Is this different from a paper jam error?
faq.a: Yes, 000020 specifically indicates a motor problem, not just stuck paper. A regular jam uses codes like E-03.

faq.q: Can I hear if the motor is working?
faq.a: Yes, during printer initialization you should hear the feed motor engage. Complete silence from the feed area suggests motor failure.

title: Wi-Fi Connection Error
shortDescription: The printer cannot connect to the specified Wi-Fi network.
fullDescription:
Error E-20 on Epson printers indicates a failure to establish a Wi-Fi connection. The printer's wireless module could not connect to the configured network, either during initial setup or when trying to reconnect to a previously saved network.

Common causes include incorrect Wi-Fi password, router out of range, network security settings blocking the printer, or the Wi-Fi network operating on 5 GHz only (most Epson printers require 2.4 GHz).

This error only affects wireless connectivity. USB and wired Ethernet connections (if available) will continue to function normally.

causes.cause: Router out of range or weak signal
Printer requires 2.4 GHz but network is 5 GHz only

symptoms: Wi-Fi icon not showing on printer

fixSteps.title: Verify Password
fixSteps.instruction: Double-check your Wi-Fi password. Passwords are case-sensitive. Re-enter it carefully during setup.

fixSteps.title: Move Printer Closer
fixSteps.instruction: Place the printer within clear line of sight of the router during setup. Move away from microwaves, cordless phones, and other interference sources.

fixSteps.title: Check 2.4 GHz Band
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure your router has the 2.4 GHz band enabled. Most Epson printers do not support 5 GHz. Check router settings if needed.

fixSteps.title: Reset Network Settings
fixSteps.instruction: On the printer, go to Settings > Network Settings > Restore Default Settings to clear saved Wi-Fi data, then retry setup.

whenToCallPro: If the printer's Wi-Fi module cannot detect any networks at all, it may have a hardware fault requiring Epson service.

faq.q: Why can't my printer find my Wi-Fi?
faq.a: Ensure you are broadcasting on 2.4 GHz, as most Epson printers do not see 5 GHz networks. Also check that SSID broadcast is enabled on your router.
Yes, if wireless setup fails, you can connect via USB cable for immediate printing while troubleshooting Wi-Fi.

title: Ink System Charging Error
shortDescription: The ink system failed to complete the initial charging process.
fullDescription:
Error code 000036 on Epson printers indicates the ink charging system could not complete its cycle. Ink charging is the process of filling the ink delivery tubes from the cartridges or tanks to the printhead during initial setup or after cartridge replacement.

This error is common on Epson EcoTank models where ink must travel from external tanks through long tubes to reach the printhead. Air bubbles in the tubes, low ink levels, or a clogged ink delivery path can prevent successful charging.

The charging process is essential for priming the printhead with ink. Without successful charging, the printer cannot produce any output.

causes.cause: Ink tanks not filled to required level

symptoms: Long charging time followed by error

fixSteps.title: Check Ink Levels
fixSteps.instruction: Verify all ink tanks are filled to the upper line. Refill any tanks that are below the lower line with genuine Epson ink.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the printer, wait 5 minutes, then turn on. The charging process will restart automatically.

fixSteps.title: Run Power Cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: If the printer allows access to maintenance functions, run Power Cleaning to force ink through the system with higher pressure.

fixSteps.title: Check for Kinked Tubes
fixSteps.instruction: If visible, inspect the ink tubes from tanks to printhead. Ensure none are kinked, pinched, or have visible air gaps.

whenToCallPro: If the ink pump has failed or the internal ink delivery system is blocked beyond what cleaning can resolve.

faq.q: How long does initial charging take?
faq.a: Initial ink charging on EcoTank models typically takes 10-20 minutes. This is normal and uses a significant amount of ink.

faq.q: Why does charging use so much ink?
faq.a: The initial charge fills the entire ink delivery system from tanks to printhead, including all internal tubes. This is a one-time high ink usage.

title: Printhead Carriage Error
shortDescription: The printhead carriage is stuck or obstructed and cannot move.
fullDescription:
Error 0xF1 on Epson printers indicates the printhead carriage cannot move along its track. This error is similar to errors on other brands where the carriage mechanism is blocked or jammed.

The carriage must travel freely from left to right on a guide rail. Paper jams, foreign objects, dried ink buildup on the rail, or a failed carriage motor can all prevent proper movement.

This error halts all printing until the obstruction is cleared. The carriage motor is protected by the firmware and will stop attempting to move if resistance is detected, to prevent damage.

causes.cause: Paper or debris blocking carriage path

fixSteps.title: Power Off and Check Carriage
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the printer. Open the top cover and gently try to move the printhead carriage left and right by hand. Note any resistance.

fixSteps.title: Remove Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove any paper scraps, packing material, or foreign objects from the carriage path. Check behind and underneath the carriage.

fixSteps.title: Clean Carriage Rail
fixSteps.instruction: Use a lint-free cloth dampened with a small amount of light machine oil to lubricate the metal carriage rail. Wipe off excess.

fixSteps.title: Check Carriage Belt
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the rubber belt that drives the carriage. If it appears loose, cracked, or has slipped off a pulley, professional repair is needed.

whenToCallPro: If the carriage belt is broken or the carriage motor has failed, professional repair is required.

faq.q: Can I move the carriage by hand?
faq.a: Yes, with the printer unplugged. Gently slide it left and right. If it moves freely, the obstruction may be intermittent.
On most Epson consumer printers, belt replacement requires significant disassembly and is best left to professionals.

title: Ink Tank Cover Open
shortDescription: The ink tank cover or cartridge cover is open or not properly latched.
fullDescription:
Error I-30 on Epson printers indicates that the ink tank cover or ink cartridge access cover is not properly closed. The printer has safety switches that detect when covers are open and will not operate until all covers are securely latched.

This error is common on EcoTank models where the ink tank unit has its own cover that must be closed separately from the main printer cover. It can also appear on cartridge-based models when the cartridge access door is ajar.

The cover detection is a safety feature to prevent ink splashing during operation and to maintain proper printhead capping when the printer is idle.

causes.cause: Ink tank cover not fully closed

fixSteps.title: Close All Covers
fixSteps.instruction: Check all covers on the printer: main scanner lid, cartridge access door, ink tank cover, and output tray flap. Close each firmly.

fixSteps.title: Check Ink Tank Cover
fixSteps.instruction: On EcoTank models, the ink tank unit has a small cover that lifts up. Ensure it is pressed down firmly until it clicks.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Cover Latch
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the cover latch mechanism is not broken or bent. Press the cover closed and verify it stays latched.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: With all covers closed, turn the printer off and on. The cover switches are re-read during startup.

whenToCallPro: If the cover is properly closed but the error persists, the cover detection switch may be faulty and need replacement.

faq.q: Which cover is causing the error?
faq.a: The error message may not specify which cover. Check all covers systematically: scanner lid, ink tank cover, cartridge door.
Yes, the small micro-switch that detects cover position can fail after extensive use. This is a simple repair for a technician.

title: Ink Tank Low Level Warning
shortDescription: One or more ink tanks are running low on ink.
fullDescription:
Error I-40 is a warning indicating one or more ink tanks are at a low level. This is a non-blocking warning on most Epson models, meaning the printer will continue to operate but is alerting the user to prepare replacement ink.

On EcoTank models, this warning is based on the ink level sensors in the transparent tank area. On cartridge-based models, it uses the cartridge chip's estimated remaining ink calculation.

Continuing to print with very low ink can cause air to enter the printhead, which can lead to clogged nozzles and print quality issues. Refilling or replacing ink promptly when warned helps maintain printhead health.

fixSteps.title: Check Ink Levels
fixSteps.instruction: View ink levels on the printer display or via Epson Status Monitor on your computer. Identify which color(s) are low.

fixSteps.title: Refill EcoTank
fixSteps.instruction: For EcoTank models: open the ink tank cover, remove the cap for the low color, and carefully pour ink from an Epson ink bottle until reaching the upper line.

fixSteps.title: Replace Cartridge
fixSteps.instruction: For cartridge models: open the cartridge access door, press the tab on the low cartridge to release it, and install a new cartridge.

fixSteps.title: Dismiss Warning
fixSteps.instruction: After refilling or replacing, the warning should clear automatically. If not, press OK or the appropriate button to acknowledge.

whenToCallPro: This warning almost never requires professional service. If ink levels seem wrong after refilling, the sensor may need recalibration.

faq.q: Can I keep printing when ink is low?
faq.a: Yes, but do not let tanks run completely empty. Running dry can allow air into the printhead and cause permanent damage.

faq.q: How much ink does an EcoTank bottle provide?
faq.a: Epson EcoTank ink bottles typically provide enough ink for thousands of pages. One bottle usually fills the tank from empty to full.

title: Wi-Fi Direct Connection Error
shortDescription: The Wi-Fi Direct (AP mode) connection has failed or cannot be established.
fullDescription:
Error W-40 on Epson printers indicates a failure in establishing a Wi-Fi Direct connection. Wi-Fi Direct allows devices to connect directly to the printer without going through a router, creating a peer-to-peer wireless link.

This error can occur when the Wi-Fi Direct feature is disabled on the printer, when the maximum number of direct connections has been reached, or when there is interference from nearby wireless networks on the same channel.

Wi-Fi Direct is useful when no router is available or when you want a direct connection for faster throughput, but it requires proper configuration on both the printer and the connecting device.

causes.cause: Wi-Fi Direct not enabled on printer

symptoms: Cannot find printer in Direct mode

fixSteps.title: Enable Wi-Fi Direct
fixSteps.instruction: On the printer, go to Settings > Network Settings > Wi-Fi Direct and ensure it is enabled. Note the network name (SSID) and password shown.

fixSteps.title: Disconnect Other Devices
fixSteps.instruction: If other devices are connected via Wi-Fi Direct, disconnect them. Most printers allow only a limited number of simultaneous direct connections.

fixSteps.title: Reset Wi-Fi Direct Settings
fixSteps.instruction: Reset Wi-Fi Direct settings from the printer menu. This generates a new SSID and password. Reconnect your device using the new credentials.

fixSteps.title: Move Devices Closer
fixSteps.instruction: Wi-Fi Direct has a shorter range than router-based Wi-Fi. Keep the connecting device within 3-5 meters of the printer.

whenToCallPro: If the Wi-Fi Direct feature does not activate after reset, the wireless module may be faulty.

faq.q: What is the difference between Wi-Fi and Wi-Fi Direct?
faq.a: Regular Wi-Fi connects through a router. Wi-Fi Direct creates a direct connection between device and printer, no router needed.

faq.q: How many devices can connect via Wi-Fi Direct?
faq.a: Most Epson printers allow up to 4-8 devices simultaneously via Wi-Fi Direct, depending on the model.

title: Carriage Encoder Error
shortDescription: The printhead carriage encoder cannot be read, preventing accurate positioning.
fullDescription:
Error code 000033 on Epson printers indicates the carriage encoder system has failed. The encoder system consists of a thin transparent strip with fine markings and an optical sensor on the carriage that reads these markings to determine precise carriage position.

When the encoder cannot be read accurately, the printer cannot position the printhead correctly for printing. This results in misaligned output or complete inability to print.

The encoder strip can become contaminated with ink mist over time, or it can be physically displaced from its mounting clips. In rare cases, the optical sensor on the carriage itself may fail.

causes.cause: Encoder strip contaminated with ink mist
Encoder strip displaced from mounting clips

symptoms: Error 000033 on display or status monitor

fixSteps.title: Locate Encoder Strip
fixSteps.instruction: Open the printer cover. Look for a thin transparent plastic strip running horizontally behind the carriage. It has very fine vertical lines printed on it.

fixSteps.title: Clean Encoder Strip
fixSteps.instruction: Gently hold the strip with one hand and wipe it with a lint-free cloth slightly dampened with distilled water. Wipe along the length of the strip, not across it.

fixSteps.title: Check Mounting Clips
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the encoder strip is properly seated in its plastic mounting clips at both ends. If a clip has released, gently reattach the strip.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle and Test
fixSteps.instruction: After cleaning, turn the printer off and on. Print a test page to verify carriage positioning is accurate.

whenToCallPro: If the encoder strip is torn or the optical sensor has failed, professional replacement is needed.

faq.q: Can I buy a replacement encoder strip?
faq.a: Yes, encoder strips are available as spare parts online. Match the part number to your specific printer model.

faq.q: How do I prevent encoder contamination?
faq.a: Regular printing keeps ink mist to a minimum. Printers in dusty environments may benefit from occasional encoder strip cleaning.
